title: Integrity Code Series - Week 7 - Hydrogen Conversion of Aging LF-ERW Pipeline: Coupled Diffusion-Fracture Life Prediction
description:
Coupled diffusion-fracture life prediction for converting a 1959-vintage 20-inch API 5L X52 low-frequency electric-resistance-welded (LF-ERW) pipeline with pre-existing selective seam corrosion pits from natural-gas to hydrogen service. The package implements a five-mechanism sequential chain:

  1. Oriani stress-assisted Fick hydrogen diffusion PDE
  2. Power-law pit growth at the ERW seam from prior NG service
  3. Murakami plus El-Haddad pit-to-crack transition
  4. Hydrogen-assisted fatigue crack growth (modified Paris)
  5. Failure criterion against hydrogen-degraded K_IC

Anchored to the 2026 PHMSA Corrective Action Order at Willow River, Minnesota and ASME B31.8/B31.12 (2026 edition) hydrogen blending requirements. 182 unit and integration tests.
references: Oriani R.A. (1970). The diffusion and trapping of hydrogen in steel. Acta Metallurgica 18: 147-157.
Murakami Y. (2002). Metal Fatigue: Effects of Small Defects and Nonmetallic Inclusions, Elsevier.
El Haddad M.H., Topper T.H., Smith K.N. (1979). Prediction of non-propagating cracks. Engineering Fracture Mechanics 11(3): 573-584.
ASME (2019). ASME B31.12 Hydrogen Piping and Pipelines.
NACE TM0316 (2016). Four-Point Bend Testing of Materials for Oil and Gas Applications.
PHMSA (2026). Corrective Action Order: Willow River, Minnesota LF-ERW rupture.
